_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 10 currently banned, 3280 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: Failed attempts at 22631 (up from 22192, +439). fail2ban total bans 3280 (up from 3237, +43). 10 IPs currently banned (up from 4). Continued botnet scanning activity from 2.57.122.x subnet and others. Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey (ED25519).

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 38 usages across 13 files: viewer.js(4), bookmarks.js(4), annotate.js(2), img2pdf.js(3), upload.js(2), merge.js(3), pages.js(4), split.js(6), redact.js(3), signatures.js(2), text-overlay.js(2), ocr.js(1), crop.js(2), forms.js(2) - all static templates, clearing operations, static SVGs, HTML entities, escaped user data (escapeHtml/escapeAttr), or integer-only interpolation. No user data injected unsafely. drawing.js and page-numbers.js have 0 innerHTML usages.

forms_module_review.innerHTML_note: 2 usages: clearing fieldEntries container (line 207), static SVG paths from switch statement (line 305). No user data in HTML.
forms_module_review.user_data_handling: Form field names from pdf.js annotations displayed via textContent (line 243). Field values from user input handled via DOM input elements, never injected into HTML. Option labels displayed via createElement('option').textContent. Download uses sanitizeFilename(). querySelector selectors use pdf.js annotation IDs (typically numeric).
forms_module_review.pdf_lib_usage: Loads PDF with ignoreEncryption:true, fills form fields via getForm()/getTextField()/getCheckBox() etc., optionally flattens. Fallback text overlay uses drawText() only.

drawing_module_review.user_data_handling: All drawing data is numerical (coordinates, stroke width, opacity). Colors from hardcoded swatches. SVG elements created via createElementNS/setAttribute DOM APIs — no innerHTML. SVG layer cleared via removeChild loop. Undo/redo uses JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) for deep cloning of numerical data.
drawing_module_review.pdf_lib_usage: Embeds drawings via page.drawLine(), drawRectangle(), drawEllipse() with numerical coordinates and validated color values.

bookmarks_module_review.innerHTML_note: 4 usages: clearing container (line 93), static SVG arrow (line 148), filter highlight via highlightMatch() with escapeHtml() (line 168), escapeHtml helper returns div.innerHTML (line 233). All safe.

redact_module_review.redaction_limitation: Draws opaque rectangles but does NOT remove underlying content streams. Hidden data may still be extractable.

annotate_module_review.innerHTML_note: 2 usages: clearing annotLayer (lines 231, 298). No user data in HTML.

jszip_min_js.purpose: ZIP file creation for split and convert module downloads

known_issues: server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.
TASK-016 redaction tool draws opaque rectangles but does NOT strip underlying content streams - redacted text/images may still be extractable from the PDF file. Users should be warned.

img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing since images are rendered via browser Image API which rejects non-images.
notes: Full security review completed - 45 checks passed, 0 critical security findings

NEW: Reviewed forms.js module (788 lines): 2 innerHTML usages (clearing container, static SVG paths) — both safe. Form field names displayed via textContent. User input handled via DOM input elements. Download uses sanitizeFilename(). No eval/Function/document.write. XSS-safe. SECURE.

NEW: Reviewed drawing.js module (943 lines): 0 innerHTML usages. All SVG via createElementNS/setAttribute DOM APIs. Drawing data entirely numerical. Undo/redo via JSON clone of numeric data. No eval/Function/document.write. XSS-safe. SECURE.
UPDATED: annotate.js now integrates drawing module (embedDrawings/hasDrawings imports). No new security concerns — same safe pattern as text-overlay and signature embedding.
UPDATED: app.js adds 'forms' to viewer container visibility toggle. No security impact.
All JS files scanned (22 in js/ + 2 in lib/): no eval(), Function(), document.write(), or unsafe patterns found
38 innerHTML usages across 13 files — all verified safe (up from 36, +2 from forms.js). drawing.js and page-numbers.js have 0 innerHTML.

SSH: Continued botnet scanning. Failed attempts at 22631 (+439 since last review). fail2ban total bans 3280 (+43). 10 IPs currently banned (up from 4). Only authorized logins from novakj via publickey (ED25519).
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (78 days remaining)
recommendations: MEDIUM: Add user-facing warning in redaction UI that underlying PDF data may not be fully removed - only visually covered.
LOW: Set server_tokens off in nginx.conf to stop leaking version info (currently exposes nginx/1.26.3)
LOW: Enable UFW firewall (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
LOW: Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for known safe files
LOW: Remove unused CGI location block from nginx config (no scripts exist)
LOW: Consider disable_symlinks on in nginx for defense in depth
